_provenance.captured_from: tests/fixtures/accounting/pendle_pt/expected_baseline.sqlite (VIB-5314 real-fork PT buy->hold->sell round-trip on Arbitrum; same DB as the ratchet fixture)
_provenance.note: Real-Anvil baseline (same DB the check-accounting-ratchet gate scores). HONEST FLOOR — PEN1/2/4/5/6 PASS (acquisition basis, entry economics, FIFO-matched realized yield in true USD via VIB-5314, lifecycle continuity, PT-quantity conservation). VIB-5319 advanced the generic cells off the typed PendleAccountingEvent: G3 PASS = the yield-ledger cell now reads PT realized_yield_usd (the PT strategy's entire realised payoff). G1 XFAIL + G6 XFAIL = the PT disposal's GROSS USD (money-trail leg G1; reconciliation proceeds sy_amount×sy_price G6) is unmeasured because the sell-side SY price is absent on this fork (sy_price=None on PT_SELL — the gateway PT/SY implied-price path, VIB-5276, same root cause as PEN3). These are MEASURED XFAILs (measured-but-blocked, not a books error and not a fabricated PASS), narrowly scoped via the pendle_pt profile's disposal_usd_unmeasured_is_xfail flag to the one ticketed null bucket — any OTHER unmeasured leg still FAILs. They flip to PASS automatically once VIB-5276 lands the sell-side SY price and the fixture is re-captured. PEN3 XFAIL = open-PT mark-to-market, same VIB-5276 gateway PT price. G11 SKIP = no failed intents in the round-trip. G14/G15/L5_22 XFAIL = Track-C / registry-mode not on for this run (same shape as every other primitive's frozen fixture).
